forecast-period.text: A high pressure system will move slowly eastwards across the Great Australian Bight during the next few days. The high will then move eastwards early next week to be centred southeast of Tasmania late Tuesday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots through the Backstairs Passage early in the morning. Winds turning southwesterly 10 to 15 knots in the middle of the day.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre around midday.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots through the Backstairs Passage during the morning and early afternoon. Winds turning southerly in the middle of the day.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly during the morning.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 2 metres through the Backstairs Passage.
